jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
The Scholarship Foundation of Santa Barbara is seeking an accomplished fundraising professional to serve as Chief Development Officer. As a member of the executive leadership team, and in conjunction with the CEO, the Chief Development Officer is SFSB’s strategic fundraising leader, focused on development strategy, high-level stakeholder engagement, stewardship of major and principal gift relationships, and identifying new philanthropic revenue opportunities.

DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES
·         Develop and implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy that includes various philanthropic efforts, including major gifts, corporate partnerships, foundation relations, planned giving, annual giving, donor stewardship, and campaign strategy to support the SFSB’s mission and strategic goals.
·         Develop and execute personalized donor engagement plans using a structured moves management approach to cultivate, solicit, and steward relationships with major and principal donors, foundations, and corporations, driving sustained and increased giving.
·         Lead and oversee the preparation of grant proposals and funding applications, ensuring timely submissions and alignment with funder priorities.
·         Partner with the CEO and Board of Directors to strengthen fundraising capacity and engagement. Support Board members in donor cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship activities.
·         Staff the Board Development Committee and provide strategic guidance on fundraising initiatives.
·         Prepare fundraising reports, forecasts, and presentations for the Board and executive leadership.
·         Work with the Chief Communications Officer on the marketing and communications efforts related to development, ensuring alignment with fundraising goals, consistent brand messaging, and compelling outreach that engages donors and elevates SFSB’s public profile.
·         Serve as a liaison with estate planning attorneys, wealth managers, and other advisors, communicating the benefits of utilizing SFSB.
·         Manage, mentor, and support direct reports with a keen focus on their professional growth, development, and performance.
·         Manage and analyze development data and donor databases to track progress, inform strategy, and ensure accurate reporting.
·         Represent SFSB at public events and engage with high-level external stakeholders to enhance visibility and build strategic partnerships.
·         Participates in ongoing training and professional development, including staying well-informed of industry trends.

BACKGROUND PROFILE
·         Deep commitment to SFSB’s mission of higher education for all. Understands the legacy of SFSB and brings a strong connection to North and South Santa Barbara County and the surrounding communities. 
·         A bachelor’s degree is required; an advanced degree and/or CFRE certification is desirable.
·         Seven to ten years of fundraising experience at the leadership level, including fundraising operations and strategy development.
·         Experience with scholarship organizations, higher education, or youth-serving nonprofits preferred.
·         A successful track record in fundraising, stewarding, and diversifying funding sources such as individual giving, events, grants, endowments, capital giving, and other partnerships.
·         Demonstrated success securing six- and seven-figure philanthropic gifts.
·         Minimum of five years of supervisory experience with a proven ability to lead, mentor, and build inclusive, high-performing teams.
·         Demonstrated success in building effective partnerships across geographically and socioeconomically diverse regions, with the cultural competency to engage and inspire a broad range of high-level stakeholders, including donors, board members, businesses, elected officials, and community leaders.
·         Exceptional verbal and written communication and storytelling; persuasive in donor communications, marketing collateral, and grant writing; able to convey complex concepts with clarity and inspiration.
·         Highly empathetic, exhibiting sound judgment, tact, and diplomacy when dealing with internal and external stakeholders.
·         Skilled in donor CRM systems and fundraising analytics (e.g., Salesforce, DonorPerfect, Kaleidoscope).
·         Capable of interpreting and communicating IRS regulations, charitable giving rules, and investment concepts to donors and stakeholders.
·         Strong team player who thrives in cross-functional environments and works well with Boards and organizational leadership.

To apply, please send your resume to:
Sarah Thompson, Senior Director, Blair Search Partners
sarah@blairsearchpartners.com

jobs.title: Director Of Development
jobs.description:
Reporting to the Vice President of Development, the Director of Development is responsible for managing a portfolio of donors and working with a team of advancement professionals responsible for securing corporate sponsors, foundation grants, and individual donations for the annual fund, specific projects, philanthropic-level membership, and the capital campaign. The Director of Development will ensure that revenue goals are met to support the mission and vision, programs, and long-term goals of the Aquarium, while expanding the donor prospect base.

Minimum starting pay for this role is $125,000 annually. Additional consideration will be given, depending on relevant qualifications and experience.

THE DIRECTOR OF DEVELOPMENT RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:
Manage an active portfolio of prospects for the annual fund, specific projects, and the capital campaign, including prospect identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship. 
Develop and engage the Trustees of the Pacific, an ambassador group comprised of influential community leaders and major donors. Develop a strategic plan with the Trustee Co-Chairs, Major Gifts Officer, and the Vice President of Development. Design quarterly meetings, engagement strategies, and ensure Trustees are motivated to fulfill roles and responsibilities. 
Develop and communicate compelling, mission-driven stories that inspire philanthropic investment by translating organizational impact into meaningful donor communications, fundraising campaigns, grant proposals, presentations, and stewardship materials.
Develop and implement specific strategies to expand the Aquarium’s donor base and oversee prospect research, analysis, and recommendations. 
Actively involve the President, CFO, Vice President of Development, Board Members, program staff, and other key volunteers in the cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship process.
Develop and manage revenue projections and expense budgets. 
Analyze fundraising data and present reports to leadership and Board Members.
Collaborate closely with the entire development department and strengthen relationships throughout the Aquarium. 
Represent and serve as an advocate for the Aquarium in the broader community. 
Supervise development staff and volunteers
Actively participate in weekend and evening Aquarium functions as needed.
Additional responsibilities may be delegated due to the culture of the Aquarium.

KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS
Excellent written communication skills.
Ability to write clear, structured, articulate, and persuasive proposals.
Excellent verbal communication and interpersonal skills.
Attention to detail, ability to meet deadlines.
Knowledge of fundraising information sources and familiarity with research techniques for fundraising prospect research.
Extraordinary organizational skills.
Strong contributor in team environments.
Excellent computer skills including but not limited to Microsoft Office programs and knowledge of SalesForce database preferred.
Ability to monitor and meet income and other defined metrics set for this position and the team.
Ability to balance “big picture” thinking with attention to detail.

QUALIFICATIONS
To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or abilities required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential job functions

Minimum five years’ experience in fundraising for non-profit institutions, preferably cultural institutions.
Minimum two to five years’ experience managing a team.
Bachelor’s degree required, master’s degree preferred.
Experience working directly with donors.
Experience working with Boards and Trustees in non-profit organizations.
Proven track record in goal setting and achievement.
Experience in handling difficult political situations
Knowledge of the institution and the community preferred.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by the individual to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Ability to sit for long periods of time.
Ability to walk, stand, bend, squat, twist.
Ability to lift and carry up to 0-25 lbs.
Ability to pull and push up to 0-25 lbs.
Full functional range of motion in all major body joints from reaching, twisting, bending, simple and power gripping, fine and gross motor skills.

MENTAL DEMANDS
The mental demands described here are representative of those that must be met by the individual to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Resilience and ability to prioritize sometimes competing tasks and work under pressure in a fast-paced environment. 
Ability to maintain positive and productive relationships with colleagues and guests, even when differing perspectives present potential for conflict.
Strong problem-solving skills to analyze issues and find effective solutions and alternatives.
Flexibility and adaptability to handle changes in schedules or responsibilities.
Critical thinking and decision-making abilities to handle complex situations.
Empathy and emotional intelligence to understand and respond to the needs of colleagues or customers.
Ability to maintain composure and handle difficult situations with professionalism.

WORK ENVIRONMENT 
The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an individual encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Normal office working conditions
Noise level in the work environment is quiet to moderate
Wet, dirty, uneven surfaces and walkways
Exposure to various temperatures, humidity, or wetness

INTERESTED & QUALIFIED?

If you meet the requirements of this position, apply on the Aquarium of the Pacific website or by clicking the apply link. You will be directed to our web application process, the successful completion of which is required by all applicants. There are THREE simple steps to this process, which is administered by Hire Select:

Upon completion of all THREE steps, your submission will be forwarded to our hiring team for consideration. If you are among the most qualified applicants, we will contact you within two to three weeks. If you do not hear from us within that time, feel free to reapply in the future as other opportunities arise. 

Thank you so much for your interest in the Aquarium of the Pacific!

jobs.title: Director of Operations & Growth
jobs.description:
Director of Operations & Growth
Company:
 Fast Lane Drive
Location:
 San Diego, California
Employment Type:
 Full-Time
Reports to:
 Founder & CEO
Career Path:
 Potential advancement to Chief Operating Officer
About Fast Lane Drive
Fast Lane Drive is a global, members-only exotic car club built around curated experiences, meaningful relationships, business networking, and philanthropy.
With chapters across the United States and internationally, FLD brings together entrepreneurs, executives, investors, collectors, and automotive enthusiasts through exclusive drives, destination experiences, charitable initiatives, and major global events.
As FLD continues to grow, we are seeking an exceptional operational leader who can work directly alongside the Founder and CEO to organize, strengthen, and scale the company.
The Opportunity
We are looking for a highly capable  Director of Operations & Growth
 to become the operational right hand and trusted second-in-command to the Founder and CEO.
This is not a traditional coordinator role. We need someone who can take ownership, make decisions, hold people accountable, solve problems, and transform the CEO’s vision into organized execution.
The ideal candidate has experience in operations, marketing, franchising, licensing, membership businesses, hospitality, luxury services, or multi-location organizations.
The mission is simple: create structure, improve execution, and allow the CEO to focus more time on strategy, partnerships, expansion, relationships, and the continued growth of the FLD brand.
Key Responsibilities
Executive and Operational Leadership
Serve as the CEO’s primary operational partner.
Turn ideas and priorities into clear plans, responsibilities, deadlines, and measurable outcomes.
Establish daily, weekly, monthly, and quarterly priorities.
Lead follow-up after meetings and ensure decisions are implemented.
Identify operational problems before they become major issues.
Make informed decisions independently while knowing when CEO approval is required.
Protect the CEO’s time by resolving issues at the appropriate level.
Provide direct, honest, and solution-oriented feedback.

Team Management and Accountability
Coordinate FLD’s operations, events, marketing, sales, technology, finance, and chapter-support teams.
Clarify roles, responsibilities, reporting structures, and ownership.
Hold team members accountable for deadlines and results.
Lead regular leadership and departmental meetings.
Create agendas, track action items, and ensure follow-through.
Identify staffing gaps and support recruiting, onboarding, training, and performance management.
Build a culture of ownership, urgency, professionalism, and communication.
Reduce dependency on the CEO for routine approvals and daily problem-solving.

Chapter, Franchise, and Licensing Operations
Support the management and expansion of FLD’s chapter network.
Build scalable onboarding systems for chapter owners and leaders.
Ensure chapters follow FLD’s brand standards, procedures, reporting requirements, and contractual obligations.
Maintain communication with chapter owners and address performance or compliance concerns.
Track membership growth, renewals, events, marketing, sponsorships, reporting, and engagement.
Improve FLD’s licensing model and support preparation for potential franchise expansion.
Work with legal and financial advisors on agreements, documentation, and compliance.
Develop manuals, playbooks, training materials, checklists, and standardized processes.
Support chapter launches, territory planning, ownership transitions, and resales.

Marketing, Sales, and Growth
Align marketing, sales, advertising, content, lead generation, and chapter-growth efforts.
Coordinate chapter content, event promotion, sponsor deliverables, and global brand initiatives.
Track leads, applications, interviews, conversions, acquisition costs, renewals, and chapter performance.
Improve communication between marketing, sales, events, and chapter owners.
Ensure marketing commitments are realistic, assigned, and delivered on time.
Support membership acquisition, retention, referrals, renewals, and reactivation.
Identify revenue opportunities through memberships, partnerships, sponsorships, events, and licensing.

Events and Member Experience
Work with the events team to improve local, national, and international experiences.
Establish event timelines, budgets, responsibilities, approvals, and post-event reporting.
Ensure events reflect the quality and exclusivity of the FLD brand.
Monitor profitability, logistics, safety, sponsor obligations, communication, and guest experience.
Support major events, including Monterey Car Week, anniversary events, international drives, and multi-chapter experiences.
Document lessons and improve future event execution.

Systems, Processes, and Reporting
Review current systems and identify opportunities to simplify or automate operations.
Improve the use of CRM, project-management, communication, event, financial, and reporting platforms.
Build dashboards for company and chapter performance.
Develop standard operating procedures across departments.
Create one centralized system for priorities, projects, deadlines, risks, and responsibilities.
Establish weekly scorecards and monthly performance reports.
Track KPIs and escalate meaningful concerns quickly.
Ensure critical information is documented and not dependent on one person.

Financial and Administrative Oversight
Work with the CEO, finance team, accountants, bookkeepers, and controller.
Support departmental, company, and event budgeting.
Monitor invoices, payments, royalties, membership revenue, sponsorship revenue, and major expenses.
Improve financial reporting from chapters and internal departments.
Support forecasting, cash-flow planning, investor reporting, and board preparation.
Identify opportunities to improve efficiency without reducing quality.

Strategic Projects
Lead company-wide projects from planning through completion.
Support expansion, partnerships, technology launches, and new revenue programs.
Coordinate projects involving multiple departments and outside partners.
Prepare recommendations and action plans for strategic decisions.
Help determine which opportunities deserve company resources.

What Success Looks Like
First 90 Days
Understand FLD’s business, team, chapters, agreements, systems, and culture.
Clarify leadership roles and reporting structures.
Establish an effective meeting and reporting rhythm.
Create one centralized accountability system.
Identify the most important operational gaps and present an action plan.
Reduce routine issues requiring direct CEO involvement.
Improve communication across departments and chapters.

First Year
Build a more organized and accountable leadership structure.
Create repeatable onboarding and operating systems.
Improve consistency across FLD’s global network.
Strengthen marketing, membership, financial, and chapter reporting.
Support measurable revenue growth while protecting the member experience.
Allow the CEO to focus more time on growth, partnerships, expansion, and key relationships.
Demonstrate the ability to advance into the Chief Operating Officer role.

Ideal Background
7–10 years of experience in operations, management, franchising, licensing, marketing, hospitality, luxury services, events, or a multi-location business.
Experience working directly with a founder, CEO, or executive team.
Proven ability to manage teams and hold senior professionals accountable.
Strong understanding of operations, marketing, sales, customer experience, and financial management.
Experience with membership, franchise, licensing, lifestyle, hospitality, automotive, or experiential businesses is highly valuable.
Ability to build structure in a fast-growing and entrepreneurial environment.
Strong financial and commercial judgment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Confidence working with entrepreneurs, executives, investors, sponsors, and high-net-worth members.
Experience with CRM, project-management, reporting, and automation tools.
Ability to travel for major events and chapter meetings.

Personal Qualities
We are looking for someone who is:
Extremely organized, dependable, and proactive.
Comfortable taking ownership and making decisions.
Direct, honest, and emotionally intelligent.
Calm and effective under pressure.
Highly responsive and consistent with follow-through.
Focused on solutions rather than excuses.
Able to respectfully challenge the CEO.
Comfortable in a fast-moving, founder-led environment.
Capable of managing strong personalities professionally.
Discreet and trustworthy.
Willing to perform both strategic and hands-on work.
Focused on the success of the entire company.

This Role Is Not a Fit for Someone Who
Needs every assignment fully defined before taking action.
Avoids difficult conversations or accountability.
Waits for the CEO to solve every problem.
Is only interested in strategy and unwilling to execute.
Struggles with changing priorities.
Cannot manage multiple projects and personalities.
Views operational problems as someone else’s responsibility.

Why Join Fast Lane Drive?
This is an opportunity to help shape the future of a growing global lifestyle and membership brand.
You will work directly with the Founder and CEO and play a central role in building the systems, team, and strategy required for FLD’s next stage of growth.
The role offers significant responsibility, growth potential, access to a unique global network, and the opportunity to become one of the company’s most important leaders.
Compensation
Competitive base salary based on experience, with performance-based compensation. Long-term incentives may be considered for an exceptional candidate who demonstrates sustained leadership and measurable impact.
How to Apply
Please submit your resume and a short introduction

jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
Incredible opportunity to be part of an amazing portfolio and team!

Based in El Segundo, CA, we are currently seeking a Development Director
who embodies our values: kind, scrappy, uplift communities, creative persistence, curiously open minded, magical places, and build great teams.

In addition to a competitive compensation package, CenterCal offers a comprehensive benefits package and company culture that is collaborative and focused on the health and well-being of its strongest asset – all employees!

Medical, dental, vision, short-term disability, long-term disability and a group term life benefit.
401k plan - Under the Company’s current benefits package, eligible employees can begin participating after 90 days of employment.
Financial advisement services through the company’s 401k advisor.
Unlimited PTO Plan
Company paid holidays
Two paid community service days – one individual volunteer day and one company-sponsored.
Flexible spending accounts and more!

GENERAL PURPOSE:
The Director will assist in all aspects of the development from deal sourcing and underwriting all the way through supporting financing, lease-up, vertical development, construction close-out and the stabilization of projects including both ground up and value-add centers.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES/DUTIES:
Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Preparing cash flow and pro forma financial models in Argus and Excel as part of underwriting process, annual plan process, and lease approval process.
Executing the business plan for value-add centers including common area renovations, re-merchandising, and supporting lease-up efforts.
Obtaining government, tenant, and other approvals required for execution of the business plan.
Performing market research including rent and sales comparables, competition assessments, market vacancy/saturation reports, zoning analysis, consumer behavior, etc. utilizing internal and external resources
Working with the Development Team to conduct detailed feasibility analysis of potential new developments including:
Evaluation and modification of financial models for retail, residential, and other products
Analysis to support the negotiation of tenant leases
Analysis of expected costs and comparison to similar projects
Review of land use issues and design constraints
Preparing investment memoranda and related reports for senior management and Investment Committee review
Coordinating the due diligence and closing process with Sellers, Lenders, 3rd party consultants, and CenterCal team members
Assisting in the coordination with architects and other consultants throughout the development process
Supporting the entitlement and permitting process by interfacing with local governments and agencies with jurisdiction
Working with the construction department to establish and maintain cost estimates to be used in pro forma models
Managing monthly development reports, debt and equity draws, and pro-forma updates
Interface with internal and external parties to generate and advance pipeline projects

Reasonable accommodations may be made to allow differently-abled individuals to perform the essential functions of the job.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:
The following are the minimum qualifications which an individual needs in order to successfully perform the duties and responsibilities of this position. Please note that the minimum qualifications may vary based upon the department size and/or geographic location.
Bachelor's degree in finance, real estate, economics or a related field. MBA or MRED preferred.
Minimum 5-7 years of full-time experience as a project manager, development manager or similar background.
Understanding of retail leasing principles including exclusives, no-build areas, co-tenancy, etc.
Cash flow modeling experience in Excel and Argus

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:

Understanding of financial underwriting techniques and investment return metrics
Ability to travel to the job site as required to perform duties
Prior financial modeling experience is required. Ability to create and update complex financial models including waterfall structures
Strong oral and written communication skills
Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills
Ability to maintain confidentiality and display good judgment
Strong attention to detail
Ability to work in a fast-paced office environment to meet deadlines and handle multiple projects simultaneously
Ability to maintain quantity without sacrificing quality
Exceptional organizational, analytical, and interpersonal skill
Extremely strong work ethic and drive to succeed

The base salary for this position starts at $175,000
per year, commensurate with experience, skills, and qualifications. In addition to base salary, this role is eligible for a discretionary bonus.

Hybrid schedule - must be in office in NYC at least 3 days per week.
Salary: $200,000-$220,000/year.
Full-Time permanent position.

Key Responsibilities

Board Relations & Governance

jobs.title: Executive Director, Corporate FP&A
jobs.description:
Condé Nast is a global media company producing the highest quality content with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers in 32 territories through print, digital, video and social platforms. The company’s portfolio includes many of the world’s most respected and influential media properties including Vogue, Vanity Fair, Glamour, GQ, The New Yorker, Condé Nast Traveler/Traveller, Allure, AD, Bon Appétit and Wired, among others.

Job Description

Location:

New York, NY

Condé Nast is a global media company producing the highest quality content with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers in 32 territories through print, digital, video, and social platforms. The company’s portfolio includes many of the world’s most respected and influential media properties, including Vogue, Vanity Fair, Glamour, GQ, The New Yorker, Condé Nast Traveler/Traveller, Allure, AD, Bon Appétit and Wired, among others.

Position Overview

The Executive Finance Director based in New York is a key member of the Corporate FP&A function, and will be responsible for leading our global Long-Range Planning (LRP), decision support and strategic projects.

While this role reports directly to the VP of Corporate FP&A, it operates as a key, on-the-ground strategic partner to the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in New York. This role requires a commercially astute, analytical, and highly articulate leader who possesses the gravitas to collaborate closely with the CFO and senior US executive stakeholders, while seamlessly maintaining alignment with the broader global finance function.

The role operates in a complex, multi-brand, matrix-structured and multi-market environment, and you will be the critical bridge between visionary strategy and grounded financial reality.

The role requires a blend of strong leadership and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence and partner effectively across multiple teams and senior leadership. The role will be tasked with translating high-level corporate ambition into robust, multi-year financial models, while simultaneously driving high-stakes strategic projects.

Driving the global long-range planning agenda requires critically assessing commercial strategies, challenging business cases, identifying risks and opportunities, and translating strategic decisions into clear financial outcomes and scenarios. The Executive Finance Director will possess the technical passion to dive deep into complex spreadsheets and data models. Being able to think macro, but execute with micro-precision is required.

While this is a senior leadership role, the person will regularly roll up their sleeves to build, audit, and refine complex financial models themselves. The role ensures the highest level of accuracy and granularity in financial data, establishing frameworks that reduce ambiguity in long-term projections.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Long Range Planning & Decision Support

Ownership of the Global LRP process: Design, orchestrate, and execute the annual 5-year global financial planning process across all brands and geographic markets, working closely with function and market teams.
Strategic Translation of Business Ideas into Financial Forecasts: Convert qualitative brand and market strategies into quantitative, accurate, and fully integrated financial forecasts (P&L & Cash Flow).
Scenario Modeling: Where necessary, be able to build sensitivity analyses and stress-test business concepts.
Serve as a trusted advisor to the business leaders and finance teams, providing clear, data-backed recommendations that influence the strategic direction of the company.
Translate complex financial data into compelling narrative presentations for executive and board-level consumption.
Confidently challenge assumptions made by commercial and brand leaders, ensuring strategic plans are both ambitious and financially viable.
Support the Business Development team, acting as the financial architect for critical, non-standard corporate initiatives—such as market entry/exit strategies, new business initiatives, brand portfolio optimization, commercial restructuring, or M&A evaluation.
Support company valuations and work closely with Advance on any support needed in this area

Financial support to Transformation Project Management Office:

Steering major transformation programs, this role will serve as the strategic financial architect and governance anchor, translating complex operational changes into measurable financial outcomes.
Partnering closely with the Transformation PMO, the role is responsible for evaluating the financial implications, risk profiles, and ROI of all proposed initiatives, ensuring every project is backed by a robust, strategically aligned business case.
Establish and oversee rigorous financial tracking frameworks and KPIs to monitor milestone-based spend, manage budget variances, and objectively validate the realization of targeted cost savings or value creation.
Provide CFO with the financial visibility to ensure the transformation projects drive sustainable, long-term profitability and are meeting their finance goals.

Commercial Financial support to Business Development Team Launching New Initiatives:

Commercial finance co-pilot to the Business Development team helping to drive the financial strategy behind high-stakes growth initiatives, brand licensing, and strategic partnerships.
Responsible for reviewing and where necessary leading the financial modeling, financial valuation, and financial risk assessment for diverse monetization strategies—including real life events and “Big Swings”—ensuring all proposals are backed by rigorous, data-driven business cases that safeguard corporate margins.
Key liaison with the financial controlling team to ensure financial implications of deals have been evaluated accurately and all risks have been highlighted.
Work closely with the business development team to support post-deal financial tracking and performance analysis, providing the executive leadership team with the critical insights and progress milestones.

Job Requirements

Ideally 10+ years experience in FP&A or strategic finance
Experience in media or a similar industry within an international company
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, experience collaborating with senior finance professionals, partnering with the business & being a ‘voice in the room’
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail, comfortable with handling lots of data and delivering quality outputs with minimal review required
Proven ability to shift effortlessly from high-level strategic thinking to granular, hands-on financial modeling.
Ability to lead through influence rather than formal authority, driving outcomes, shaping direction, and supporting the success of colleagues and the wider organisation.
Strong relationship-building skills and ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders across functions and levels
Builds effective relationships across diverse cultures, languages, working styles, and functions, recognising and adapting to differing perspectives and priorities.
Able to manage business change in a rapidly changing environment, both in the company and the wider media industry
Able to work in a cross-functional matrix organisation, and experience in managing work deliverables across multiple time-zones.
Works well under pressure prioritises effectively and takes ownership of deliverables
Self-motivated, flexible & a great team player, adaptable to pitch in to wider demands of the team where required
Proficient user of G-suite and prior exposure to Oracle EPM Advanced is an advantage.

The expected base salary range for this position is from $270,000-290,000. Salary offers are based on a wide range of factors including but not limited to relevant skills, training, experience, and education.

What happens next?

jobs.description:
Job Description

The Assistant Director of Communications for Content provides editorial leadership including editorial planning and content development across digital and print platforms, publication management and team/project collaboration. This position participates in overall communications strategy, manages a number of publications including the Maxwell Perspective, the school’s flagship magazine, supervises freelance writers, graphic designers and photographers, as well as students.

This position is part of a bargaining unit and is represented by the union SEIU, Local 200United.

Education and Experience

Bachelor’s degree in English language arts, marketing, communications or related field from an accredited university.
Five years of experience with increasing levels of responsibility for reporting, writing and editing feature stories and other types of content for distribution in digital and print media.

Skills and Knowledge

Strong understanding of current industry standards and techniques for digital content creation and management; knowledge of best practices for UX, social media, SEO and online lead development strategies.
Demonstrated ability to plan, oversee, and measure the execution of successful content marketing strategies across multiple channels.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively to establish new systems and protocols and ensure successful adoption.
Familiarity with processes and protocols for print production, partnering and collaborating with designers and printers on the execution.
Excellent project management skills; must be able to work collaboratively to complete projects on deadline and within budget.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills and highly collaborative nature; ability to facilitate work between a wide range of individuals and groups within Maxwell and across campus.
Experience with Adobe Creative Suite, including InDesign and Photoshop, for the purpose of helping to create banners, buttons and other elements as needed is desirable, but not required.

Responsibilities

Editorial Leadership

Leads editorial planning and content development across digital and print platforms.
Oversees an editorial calendar and coordinates daily communications activities, serving as the primary writer, editor and publisher of 150+ annual school news and feature stories such as research articles, profiles, gift announcements and event coverage in support of student recruitment, alumni engagement, giving, peer rankings and other initiatives.
Ensures accuracy, clarity, tone alignment and adherence to Syracuse University editorial and brandling guidelines.
Oversees pre-publication editorial review process with internal and external stakeholders such as deans, department chairs, directors, University partners and major donors.
Serves as lead editorial contact for campus-based partners in other schools and colleges, university communications, and central marketing, ensuring materials generated within and outside the school are leveraged for maximum exposure and impact and to ensure consistent messaging, brand alignment and high editorial standards.
Collaborates with University marketing partners including the University’s head of photography to conceptualize and execute cover shoots as needed for high-visibility publications and to make certain Maxwell publications meet branding standards.
Leads school publications from concept to completion, including the school’s flagship magazine, Maxwell Perspective, which is mailed to over 38,000 parents, alumni, donors and friends annually, as well an annual Impact Report and a monthly newsletter that support the school’s reputation and rankings among peer evaluators.
Develops themes and content based on strategic goals in a multi-level editorial process that includes school leadership and University partners.
Coordinates project timelines, ensures adherence to deadlines and oversees external vendor contracts and budgeting.
Collaborates with dean’s office colleagues, including development staff, to cultivate strategic outreach and mailing lists.
Ensures accuracy, tone, adherence to University editorial style and branding, as well as alignment with strategic goals.

Team Collaboration and Project Coordination

Assists the Director of Communications and Media Relations in executing the Maxwell School’s communications strategy.
Provide editorial and storytelling expertise to translate strategic goals into actionable communication plans and campaigns that support enrollment, reputation-management, giving and stakeholder engagement.
Work collaboratively within school’s communications leadership team to develop and implement communications that support and enhance Maxwell’s global reputation in public affairs.
Collaborate with Director and Associate Director to collect data and metrics other metrics to inform strategy and best practices across digital and print channels.
As part of the broader external affairs team that includes development, alumni relations and graduate recruitment, provides support and editorial expertise for high-profile events and publications.

Vendor Contract and Student Oversight

Oversees freelance photographers, writers, graphic designers and other external vendors on behalf of the communications and media relations team.
Manages contracts, collaborates with University purchasing and disbursement offices to collect estimates and ensure timely payment and coordinates with school budget officer and director of communications to ensure adherence to school budget.
Oversees vendor assignments, workflow, editorial review, revisions and resulting content organization while ensuring adherence to deadlines and strategic alignment.
Supervises a team of part-time student writers who create content for the school’s digital and print channels.
Provide assignments and manage workflow and deadlines, edit resulting content and lead internal review and publication process to ensure accuracy and adherence to editorial standards and branding.
Uphold and strengthen the University’s and the School’s commitment to equity, inclusion and belonging. Identify and participate in workshops and training on best practices for communicating with diverse audiences.

Physical Requirements

Not Applicable

Tools/Equipment

Not Applicable

Application Instructions

In addition to completing an online application, please attach a resume and cover letter.

About Syracuse University

Syracuse University is a private, international research university with distinctive academics, diversely unique offerings, and an undeniable spirit. Located in the geographic heart of New York State, with a global footprint, and over 150 years of history, Syracuse University offers a quintessential college experience.

The scope of Syracuse University is a testament to its strengths: a pioneering history dating back to 1870; a choice of more than 200 majors, 100 minors, and 200 advanced degree programs offered across the University’s 13 schools and colleges; over 15,000 undergraduates and over 6,000 graduate students; more than a quarter of a million alumni in 160 countries; and a student population from all 50 U.S. states and 123 countries. For more information, please visit http://www.syracuse.edu.

About The Syracuse Area

Syracuse is a medium-sized city situated in the geographic center of New York State approximately 250 miles northwest of New York City. The metro-area population totals approximately 500,000. The area offers a low cost of living and provides many social, cultural, and recreational options, including parks, museums, festivals, professional regional theater, and premier shopping venues. Syracuse and Central New York present a wide range of seasonal recreation and attractions ranging from water skiing and snow skiing, hiking in the Adirondacks, touring the historic sites, visiting wineries along the Finger Lakes, and biking on trails along the Erie Canal.

EEO Statement

Syracuse University is an equal-opportunity institution. The University prohibits discrimination and harassment based on race, color, creed, religion, sex, gender, national origin, citizenship, ethnicity, marital status, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity and gender expression, veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law to the extent prohibited by law. This nondiscrimination policy covers admissions, employment, and access to and treatment in University programs, services, and activities.

Commitment to Supporting and Hiring Veterans

Syracuse University has a long history of engaging veterans and the military-connected community through its educational programs, community outreach, and employment programs. After World War II, Syracuse University welcomed more than 10,000 returning veterans to our campus, and those veterans literally transformed Syracuse University into the national research institution it is today. The University’s contemporary commitment to veterans builds on this historical legacy, and extends to both class-leading initiatives focused on making an SU degree accessible and affordable to the post-9/11 generation of veterans, and also programs designed to position Syracuse University as the employer of choice for military veterans, members of the Guard and Reserve, and military family members.

Commitment to a Respectful and Welcoming Community

Syracuse University fosters a welcoming learning environment where students, faculty, administrators, staff, curriculum, social activities, governance, and all aspects of campus life reflect a broad range of perspectives and experiences. The University community values the many similarities and differences among individuals and groups. At Syracuse, we are committed to preparing students to engage with and appreciate the richness of backgrounds, beliefs, and experiences that shape our society. To achieve this, we strive to cultivate a community that respects and encourages open dialogue, understanding, and mutual respect.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Lead with Purpose. Inspire Hope. Transform Lives.

Culinary Angels is seeking an experienced, visionary, and compassionate Executive Director to lead our growing nonprofit organization into its next chapter of service and impact.

For more than ten years, Culinary Angels has provided nutrient-rich meals and nutrition education—at no cost—to individuals undergoing cancer treatment and to their caregivers throughout the Tri-Valley community. Powered by dedicated volunteers, generous donors, and strong community partnerships, we believe that nourishing the body is an essential part of healing.

As Executive Director, you will partner closely with the Board of Directors to provide strategic leadership, strengthen organizational sustainability, expand community partnerships, and advance our mission of serving those facing a cancer challenge. This is an exceptional opportunity for a collaborative leader who values transparency, integrity, innovation, and community engagement.

Key Responsibilities

The Executive Director provides overall leadership for the organization and is responsible for:

Implementing the organization's strategic vision and priorities in partnership with the Board of Directors.
Leading day-to-day operations while fostering a culture of collaboration, accountability, compassion, and excellence.
Ensuring sound financial stewardship, organizational compliance, and effective risk management.
Developing sustainable fundraising strategies and cultivating relationships with individual donors, foundations, corporations, and government partners.
Building strong partnerships with healthcare providers, community organizations, volunteers, and supporters.
Serving as the organization's chief spokesperson and ambassador throughout the community.
Recruiting, developing, and inspiring a high-performing staff and volunteer team committed to exceptional service.

Qualifications

The successful candidate will bring:

A minimum of seven years of progressively responsible nonprofit executive or senior leadership experience.
Demonstrated success in strategic planning, organizational leadership, fundraising, financial management, and Board relations.
Experience leading organizational growth and managing change.
Outstanding communication, relationship-building, and public speaking skills.
High emotional intelligence, diplomacy, and the ability to build trust with diverse stakeholders.
A collaborative leadership style grounded in integrity, accountability, transparency, and service.
Passion for improving the lives of individuals and families experiencing serious health challenges.
Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.

Why Culinary Angels?

This is more than a leadership position—it is an opportunity to make a lasting difference in the lives of individuals and families navigating one of life's most difficult journeys. Every meal delivered represents hope, compassion, and community.

If you are an innovative, mission-driven leader who believes in the power of collaboration, service, and community impact, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of Culinary Angels.

To Apply: Please submit the following items to 
maureen@culinaryangels.org

Cover letter describing your interest and qualifications
Current resume
Three professional references

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

﻿ Culinary Angels is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applicants from diverse backgrounds and experiences who share our commitment to serving the community with compassion, dignity, and respect.

Advanced degree from an accredited school, preferably in Business, Public Administration, Government Affairs or related field.

Background in building and mentoring a high-performance management team in a complex organization with demonstrable skill in developing strong teams and collaborations.
Knowledge of how to develop and maintain relationships with key stakeholders and a wide range of business, industry, and community partners.
Experience managing grants and budgets.

Experience with program design, implementation and operations.

Excellent interpersonal skills.

Ensures community maintains appropriate staffing levels to meet the needs of residents and in accordance with applicable legal requirements.
Attracts, develops, engages, and retains associates in accordance with Company policies. Responsible for recruiting and hiring high quality and engaged associates and ensuring they are appropriately trained and developed to meet the needs of residents. Reviews promotions, development plans, disciplinary actions, and termination decisions ensuring consistency in the selection and retention of quality associates. Analyzes trends in recruiting, turnover, and retention to continually improve community performance.
Builds high degree of resident satisfaction and retention. Is responsible for maintaining positive resident relations and is accessible and approachable to residents and their families. Is proactive in solving problems and resolving issues with support from district leaders. Leads efforts to leverage satisfied residents and families to grow community occupancy. Partners with the Resident Council as necessary. Administers resident satisfaction and other surveys and works with district team to create plan to address opportunities for improvement in resident satisfaction, experience, and engagement.
For assisted living or memory care communities, creates collaborative relationship with community clinical leader to ensure community’s care and services are appropriate to meet the needs of residents. Oversees resident services including the admission process, healthcare management, and maintenance of resident documentation to ensure high quality services and compliance with Company policy and applicable legal requirements. Assists in developing and conducting service plan reviews, consistent with applicable legal requirements, with appropriate clinical care team members and resident families to maintain the personal dignity of residents.
Demonstrates a high degree of financial acumen as it relates to community operations; works to meet or exceed budgeted revenue and profitability; and proactively creates plans to overcome unanticipated expenses or revenue shortfalls. Continually explores means of revenue enhancement and expense reduction, while meeting the needs of residents and adhering to Company policies and applicable regulations.
Drives sales and marketing efforts in collaboration with community sales leader to meet or exceed occupancy or revenue targets by developing new business, generating leads, and building strategic relationships. Engages with prospects who tour the community. Identifies trends and implements approved sales and marketing activities and strategies to maximize revenue (RevPAR).
Identifies and builds positive relationships with local influencers and professionals to raise community profile. Becomes active in social and civic affairs of the local community that align with the Company’s mission. Represents the community and the Company to governmental agencies (as appropriate), professional organizations, community groups, and other appropriate agencies and groups.
Ensures buildings, grounds, and property are up to Company standards through the oversight of preventative maintenance systems and programs and frequent inspections that meet Company standards of excellence.
Enforces current Company policies and procedures. Maintains applicable licenses in accordance with Company, Federal, State, and local requirements.

This job description represents an overview of the responsibilities for the above referenced position. It is not intended to represent a comprehensive list of responsibilities. An associate should perform all duties as assigned by their supervisor.

Qualifications

Education and Experience

High school diploma or GED required. Bachelor’s Degree preferred. Minimum of two years in operational leadership experience required. Additional years of experience can be substituted for the education requirement on a year-for-year basis.

Certifications, Licenses, And Other Special Requirements

Must have a valid administrator license in states where required. Must have a valid driver’s license and access to a private vehicle for business use.

Management/Decision Making

Uses analytic skills and understanding of the organization and the business in order to handle arising problems and issues. Demonstrates leadership skills to be responsible for the overall direction, coordination, and evaluation of resident care, marketing, food service, housekeeping, and maintenance units. Carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the Company’s policies and applicable laws.

Knowledge And Skills

Has knowledge of the organization, industry, and a functional discipline. Extensive understanding of technical areas gained through experience and used to complete and/or oversee assignments. Familiarity with Microsoft software preferred. Effective written and oral communications skills are essential. Ability to prioritize and effectively manage multiple tasks is essential. Ability to delegate assignments to the appropriate individuals based on their skills, roles, and interests. Demonstrates good judgment and strong problem solving and decision making skills.

Physical Demands and Working Conditions

Standing
Walking
Sitting
Use hands and fingers to handle or feel
Reach with hands and arms
Stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl
Talk or hear
Ability to lift: up to 50 pounds
Vision
Requires interaction with co-workers, residents or vendors
Occasional weekend, evening or night work if needed to ensure shift coverage
On-Call on an as needed basis
Possible exposure to communicable diseases and infections
Potential injury from transferring, repositioning, or lifting residents
Exposure to latex
Possible exposure to blood-borne pathogens
Possible exposure to various drugs, chemical, infectious, or biological hazards
Requires Travel: Occasionally
Requires Driving: Drives self (Tier 2)

Brookdale is an equal opportunity employer and a drug-free workplace.

About Us

Brookdale is a Great Place to Be :

Gracious hospitality and neighborliness for our residents and families.
Home-like feel and all-around comfort for residents and visiting family members.
Entertaining events and gatherings so our residents can find connections in groups and 1:1 settings.
Industry leader in clinical care.
Nationwide company with 589 communities as of November 30, 2025, offering many opportunities to grow and learn as a sales professional.
Extensive corporate support including a robust training program.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Wealth Strategies Group - Wirehouse & Independent Broker-Dealers, South Region
jobs.description:
Morgan Stanley Investment Management (MSIM)
is one of the largest global asset management organizations of any full-service securities firm, with more than 50 years of history, a presence in 26 countries, and a total of $1.9 trillion in assets under management as of March 31, 2026.

MSIM provides broad-based specialization across a range of asset classes in public and private markets. Our independent investment teams leverage Morgan Stanley’s global resources to serve a diverse client base of governments, institutions, corporations, advisors and individuals worldwide. Our investment solutions include a broad range of alternatives, high-conviction active strategies, customization strategies, ESG expertise and tax management solutions.  For further information about Morgan Stanley Investment Management, please visit  www.morganstanley.com/im

The Wealth Strategies Group
is the sales distribution function for advanced strategies for High-Net-Worth (HNW) Investors within Eaton Vance as part of Morgan Stanley Investment Management, the asset management division of Morgan Stanley. This group of Specialists focus on the HNW tax suite of solutions including but not limited to; Customized SMA’s in Equity and Fixed income, the Exchange fund, derivatives, and charitable solutions.

The Wealth Strategy Specialist is responsible for representation of product distribution within the Wirehouse and Independent Broker-Dealer channels in the South Region. Collaborating closely with Relationship Managers, Regional Rep Counterparts, marketing, and additional internal resources, the Wealth Strategy Specialist owns client experience for WSG related products within their territory, ensuring the highest level of service and client satisfaction. Act in a consultative manner with sophisticated Advisors in implementing advanced strategies, increasing AUM of major Wirehouse and Independent Broker-Dealer clients.

Responsibilities

Responsible for representation of product distribution at Wirehouse and Independent Broker-Dealers within the South Region – TX, OK, AK, LA, MS and AL.
Product Subject Matter Expert across customized portfolios, tax management and specialized strategies. Products include but not limited to: Parametric Equity and Income SMA’s, Derivatives, EV Exchange Funds, EV Charitable solutions.
Identify client needs and coordinate efforts to service assigned advisors.
Provide technical and sales support when possible and partner with Eaton Vance wholesalers inside territory footprint.
Exhibit proficiency in meeting with advisors and their largest clients.
Conduct strategy training meetings at branch and regional level for advisors and regional management of partner firms.
Work in conjunction with an Internal Specialist to fulfill the business plan for the territory.
Understand and follow the Strategy Specialist guidelines.

Requirements

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent.
Minimum 10 years financial services experience, with 5 years of sales experience preferred.
Strong technical background and familiarity in advanced investment strategies for HNW clients.
FINRA Series 7, 63 or 66 required
Additional investment designations such as CFP and CAIA strongly preferred.
Highly motivated and a self-starter with proven leadership skills.
Proven ability to work independently with measurable results in a fast paced, challenging environment.
Must be able to demonstrate proficiency in communicating esoteric and complex strategies.
Ability to drive results, positively influence and leverage relationships.
Must be able, when requested, to work collaboratively with other EV wholesalers or specialists.
Must be able to handle consistently active rotation and frequent travel schedule.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Must be extremely motivated and have demonstrated sales success and job performance.
Must be comfortable with technology.

Who You Are

Operates with a strong sense of ethics and integrity in your interactions with others and decision making.
Takes accountability and ownership in your work with a focus on long-term value creation.
Track record of fostering a culture of honesty, transparency and accountability within teams.
Proven ability to collaborate across teams to deliver optimal solutions for clients.
A passion for innovation, with a continuous improvement mindset to drive excellence.
Respect for individual differences, with an openness to diverse perspectives.
Engagement in community service, volunteering or corporate citizenship initiatives.
Commitment to mentoring and supporting professional growth of others.

What You Can Expect From Morgan Stanley

At Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work.

To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices into your browser.

Expected base pay rates for the role will be between $125,000 and $300,000 per year at the commencement of employment. However, base pay if hired will be determined on an individualized basis and is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include commission earnings, incentive compensation, discretionary bonuses, other short and long-term incentive packages, and other Morgan Stanley sponsored benefit programs.

Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunity employer committed to building and maintaining a workforce that is diverse in experience and background. Our recruiting efforts reflect our strong commitment to a culture of inclusion, where individuals are hired, developed, and advanced based on their skills and talents.

Our workforce reflects a broad cross-section of the global communities in which we operate, bringing a variety of backgrounds, talents, perspectives, and experiences.

For more information, please visit: https://www.morganstanley.com/people-opportunities/eeo.

jobs.title: Executive Director of Communities
jobs.description:
Department:
Oak Cliff Bible Fellowship (OCBF)
The Executive Director of Kingdom Communities provides strategic leadership for a unified ecosystem of Fellowship and Outreach. This role is responsible for cultivating an environment where the body of believers is empowered to create meaningful experiences that drive spiritual development and advance the Kingdom Agenda. Rather than focusing solely on centralized programming, this department takes a community-forward approach, supporting the organic ministry and mission initiated by the congregation both within the church and throughout the broader community.
Core Responsibilities Ministerial Responsibilities & Spiritual Care

Demonstrate a personal commitment to Jesus Christ and alignment with the philosophy, ministry, and vision of Oak Cliff Bible Fellowship.
Support the spiritual, social, emotional, and physical well-being of the church family through prayer, presence, and ministry engagement.
Provide prayer, spiritual assistance, and ministerial support to coworkers and individuals encountered in the course of work.
Communicate and model biblical principles during staff meetings, ministry activities, and church events.
Model a lifestyle consistent with biblical principles and the church’s core values.
Engage in ongoing spiritual discipleship while faithfully carrying out essential job responsibilities in support of the church’s mission to disciple believers and impact the world.

Strategic Leadership & Ministry Alignment

Lead the overall vision, strategy, and execution of Kingdom Communities, integrating Fellowship and Outreach into a unified, high-impact ministry.
Establish a clear and compelling framework for how individuals engage in community, discipleship, and service across the church and external outreach efforts.
Align Kingdom Communities with the broader organizational vision, ensuring consistency with the church’s mission, values, and strategic priorities.
Design and oversee a comprehensive community engagement model that connects individuals into meaningful relationships and spiritual growth opportunities.
Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to community engagement, discipleship, volunteer involvement, and outreach impact.

Community Engagement & Kingdom Impact

Design and oversee a comprehensive community engagement model that connects individuals into meaningful relationships and spiritual growth opportunities.
Ensure clear pathways exist for individuals to move from attendance to active participation in Kingdom Communities.
Champion an environment where individuals are known, loved, valued, spiritually developed, and commissioned.
Provide strategic oversight for outreach initiatives, ensuring alignment with the church’s mission to impact the broader community.
Foster partnerships and initiatives that create meaningful opportunities for service, evangelism, and Kingdom impact.
Ensure outreach efforts are both spiritually impactful and operationally effective.

Team Leadership & Development

Lead, develop, and coach directors and ministry leaders within Kingdom Communities to build a strong leadership pipeline.
Establish clear expectations, accountability, and performance standards for leaders and
teams.
Equip leaders to effectively shepherd their teams while driving ministry outcomes.
Foster a culture of collaboration, unity, and shared ownership across ministries.
Operate as an enterprise leader, contributing to ministry-wide alignment and decision-making.

Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree or theological training preferred.
10+ years of progressive leadership experience in ministry, community engagement, or organizational leadership.
Proven ability to lead large, complex functions or ministries with multiple teams and leaders.
Strong strategic thinking and execution capabilities.
Experience in discipleship, outreach, and community-building initiatives.
Demonstrated ability to develop leaders and build high-performing teams.
Excellent communication and relational leadership skills.

Every team member plays a role in advancing our mission, vision, and values. We strive to reflect Christ in all we do, and we ask each employee to model biblical character, professionalism, integrity, and a spirit of service in every interaction.
This job description is intended to describe the general nature and level of work performed and is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities.

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
Reports to:
Executive Director 
Position Status:
Corpus Christi, TX |
Hybrid/Remote within South Texas |
Full-time

Position Overview & Basic Function: 

Foster Angels of South Texas (FAST) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving the lives of children in foster care. For more than 25 years, Foster Angels has been meeting the immediate and long-term needs of children, youth, and families connected to foster care across 19 counties in South Texas. Whether providing emergency assistance, educational support, life skills, post adoption services, or helping create moments of normalcy, our mission is simple: say “yes” when children need us most. FAST is searching for an experienced fundraiser and relationship builder to help us write the next chapter. This is more than a fundraising position. It’s an opportunity to share our mission with the community and give them the chance to help transform the lives of children throughout South Texas. Success in this role requires an active presence in the community through donor meetings, relationship-building, sponsorship development, and collaboration with community partners.

About the Role:

The Director of Philanthropy is responsible for leading Foster Angels’ fundraising and donor engagement efforts throughout South Texas. Working closely with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, and program staff, this individual will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners while helping diversify and grow the organization’s philanthropic support. 

This position requires someone who enjoys building relationships, connecting with people, and representing Foster Angels throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, and Laredo. 

Primary Responsibilities: 

Donor Development and Stewardship

Cultivate and steward relationships with current and prospective donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners.
Proactively identify new donor and sponsorship opportunities throughout South Texas.
Develop strategies to grow annual giving, recurring gifts, major gifts and unrestricted support.
Work closely with the Executive Director on donor cultivation, solicitations, and stewardship.

Fundraising Strategy and Campaigns

Develop and manage an annual fundraising plan with clear goals, timelines, and prospect lists.
Lead fundraising strategy for the Champions of Courage Luncheon including identifying and securing a presenting sponsor.
Partner with regional staff to support sponsorships, logistics, tracking, and stewardship for the Rio Grande Valley Luncheon and other events. 
Coordinate fundraising efforts for: Coastal Bend Day of Giving, Back-to-School Shoe Drive, Holiday Gift Drive for Teens, Heart Gallery initiatives, and third-party and community fundraising campaigns.

Regional Fundraising

Work collaboratively with regional staff to expand philanthropic support throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, Laredo and surrounding counties.
Build upon existing relationships in the Rio Grande Valley without duplicating the work of regional staff.
Help develop new donor and corporate relationships in Laredo and other areas where Foster Angels has fewer established connections.

Grants and Foundation Support

Research grant opportunities aligned with Foster Angels’ programs and priorities. 
Maintain a grant calendar with deadlines, reporting requirements, and award information.
Prepare grant proposals, letters of inquiry, supporting documents, and reports.
Coordinate with program and finance staff to gather accurate data, budgets, outcomes, and stories.
Maintain strong relationships with current and prospective foundation partners.

Qualifications:

A commitment to Foster Angel’s mission to help children in foster care.     
Three or more years of experience in fundraising, nonprofit development, major gifts, corporate sponsorships or a related field. Equivalent combinations of education and relevant experience will be considered.
Demonstrated success building and maintaining professional relationships.
Self motivated with the ability to prioritize responsibilities and manage time effectively.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Comfortable initiating meetings, making direct asks, and following up consistently.
Ability to work independently while remaining collaborative and accountable.
An outgoing, people-oriented personality. 
Microsoft Office & Google Suite proficiency. 

Preferred Knowledge & Skills:

Experience working in nonprofit, child welfare, education, or another mission-driven organization.
Experience securing corporate sponsorships, individual gifts, or foundation support.
Grant writing and reporting experience.
Familiarity with Bloomerang, Qgiv, or Canva.
Knowledge of the South Texas business and philanthropic community.
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.

Other 

Background check; must have reliable transportation, valid driver’s license, and willingness to travel throughout South Texas.
Commitment to work-life balance with collaborative, knowledge sharing and supportive team dynamic 
This position may work remotely for a significant portion of the week, but regular in-person donor meetings, community events, staff collaboration, and occasional regional travel are required. Limited evening availability expected for a few events per year. 

Compensation and Benefits

Salary: Competitive salary and benefits commensurate with experience, qualifications, and demonstrated fundraising success.

Benefits include:
We believe great fundraising happens by building relationships, not by sitting behind a desk. Foster Angels offers a flexible work environment built on trust, accountability, and results while supporting a healthy work-life balance. 
Paid time off and paid holidays
Simple IRA retirement plan
Partial employer-paid health insurance
Mileage reimbursement for approved travel
Professional development opportunities
Supportive and collaborative team culture

If you're passionate about building relationships and making a lasting impact for children and families across South Texas, we’d love to hear from you:
Please submit your resume and cover letter to info@fosterangelsstx.org. Please reference “Director of Philanthropy INTEREST” in the Subject line. The deadline to apply is July 31st.
