new_jobs.title: Chief Program Officer, EdRedesign Lab
new_jobs.description:
Company Description

By working at Harvard University, you join a vibrant community that advances Harvard's world-changing mission in meaningful ways, inspires innovation and collaboration, and builds skills and expertise. We are dedicated to creating a diverse and welcoming environment where everyone can thrive.

Why join the Harvard Graduate School of Education?

The Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE) is a diverse community of learners, teachers, and employees who are passionate about changing the world through education and striving for maximum impact in the field of education.

Many choose to work at the Harvard Graduate School of Education because they believe in our mission and are excited by our vision for the future. We have a reputation as a great place to work, for our excellent leadership, and we are a strong community that values diversity. For more information about HGSE, its programs, research, and faculty, please visit: www.gse.harvard.edu.

Job Description

Job Summary:

The EdRedesign Lab at the Harvard Graduate School of Education seeks an inaugural Chief Program Officer (CPO) to serve as a member of the organization’s Executive Leadership Team. The CPO will oversee the design, implementation, growth, and impact of EdRedesign’s flagship talent development programs and field-building initiatives supporting 70+ communities nationwide to develop cradle-to-career systems of opportunity that place millions of young people and families on pathways to educational attainment and economic mobility.

Reporting to the Executive Director, the CPO will catalyze programmatic innovations, cultivate a high-performing and collaborative team culture, contribute to organizational strategy and fundraising, and help position EdRedesign as a leading national hub in US higher education advancing the cradle-to-career place-based partnership field. This role is ideal for a mission-driven leader who combines strategic vision with operational excellence and is passionate about improving outcomes for children, youth, and families through collective impact across education, health, workforce, housing, social services, and related community development systems.

Job-Specific Responsibilities:
Strategic Leadership and Oversight of Flagship Talent Development Programs

The Chief Program Officer will provide strategic leadership and oversight to EdRedesign’s program team that lead the day-to-day of the organization’s flagship talent development programs and field-building initiatives, including:

Institute for Success Planning — supporting cross-sector collaborations across the nation to develop relationship-based personalized systems of support for young people and families.
EdRedesign Fellowship for Cradle-to-Career Partnership Leaders — supporting the comprehensive leadership development of the next wave of senior leaders of cradle-to-career place-based partnerships.
By All Means Senior Fellows Program — a visiting fellowship for exemplary senior leaders of place-based collaborative action.
Student Pathways Programming — advancing strategies to enroll Harvard students and emerging young professionals across the nation into careers in the cradle-to-career place-based field.
Emerging Talent Development Initiatives — emerging efforts to build replicable talent development infrastructure within Harvard Graduate School of Education degree programs, HGSE professional education and the broader Harvard University ecosystem. The development of strategic partnerships with mission-aligned US higher education institutions and national field-leading talent development programs and organizations.

Key program-level responsibilities:

Provide strategic leadership, coaching, mentoring and professional development support to the EdRedesign program team.
Provide strategic financial stewardship and oversight to all of EdRedesign’s flagship talent development programs
Lead the development and execution of a multi-year strategy to refine EdRedesign’s programmatic priorities, strengthen and scale flagship programs, and ensure alignment with the organization’s field-building objectives.
Play a leading role in ensuring effective cross-departmental collaboration between EdRedesign’s program team and the research, communications, finance, and operations teams.
Ensure program quality, innovation, and measurable impact across all offerings.
Dedicate time and space with the programs team to pursue innovative practices, pilot new ideas, and adapt programming to emerging needs and sector-wide demand in the field. Identify opportunities for program enhancements, and strategic partnerships to fill gaps in the talent development landscape.

Organization-wide Leadership and Cross-Departmental Alignment

Serve as a member of the Executive Leadership Team that sets the agenda for organization-wide strategic planning, resource allocation, and decision-making.
Serves as the designated second-in-command on behalf of the Executive Director in their absence, providing leadership and decision-making authority across the organization's programmatic and operational functions.
Work in close collaboration with EdRedesign’s Executive Team and senior staff to shape cross-departmental alignment, organizational culture, staff professional development, and the development of systems of operational excellence.
Work in close collaboration with EdRedesign’s Executive Team and senior staff to develop an organization-wide learning and professional development agenda.
Champion a culture of commitment to EdRedesign’s core values: equity, partnership, humility, vision, and learning.

Strategic Partnerships, Fundraising and External Relations

Develop and steward strategic partnerships that advance EdRedesign's mission and expand its impact. Identify new opportunities to expand the footprint of the cradle-to-career place-based partnership field with national nonprofits, higher education institutions, national networks, national service programs, community-based organizations, local governments, school districts, and related spheres of influence.
Provide strategic leadership, oversight and coverage to a subset of EdRedesign’s special projects, with a particular focus on emerging programming related to federal, state, and local policy implementation.
Work in close collaboration with EdRedesign’s senior staff to deepen strategic partnerships across the Harvard University ecosystem with mission-aligned centers, programs, initiatives, faculty, and staff.
Partner with the Executive Director and development team to secure philanthropic support for programs and organizational growth.
Contribute to EdRedesign funding proposals for relevant signature initiatives and external engagements
Provide strategic support to donor cultivation, proposal development, reporting, and stewardship activities.
Serve as a spokesperson for EdRedesign in key venues that advance the organization’s thought leadership and field-level contributions, including major conferences and community events. Periodically deliver presentations, keynotes, and related talks. Participate in relevant panel discussions that advance national field-building.
Participate in national working groups, steering committees, and related field-building efforts.

Working Conditions:

EdRedesign is a hybrid work environment with some staff working in the office at least part of each work week while other staff are primarily remote. The Chief Program Officer may opt for either work arrangement in consultation with the Executive Director.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications:

Master's degree or equivalent experience in education, public policy, social work, nonprofit management, public administration, social entrepreneurship, or related field.
Minimum of 12 years of progressive senior leadership experience managing complex programs, networks, or organizations.
Demonstrated experience leading cross-sector initiatives involving education, government, nonprofit, philanthropy, and community stakeholders.
Experience supervising and developing high-performing teams.
Experience managing multi-million-dollar budgets with significant regional and/or national-level reach.
Ability to travel for national field-building events, funder meetings, and related programming and national events.

Additional Qualifications and Skills:

Strong commitment to the mission, vision, and values of The EdRedesign Lab.
National reputation and recognition in cross-sector collaboration, public policy, education, and/or related fields.
Strong strategic planning, project management, and organizational leadership skills.
The ability to represent EdRedesign, the Harvard Graduate School of Education, and Harvard University both internally and externally.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills.
Experience leading collective impact initiatives at the federal, state, and/or local levels.
Experience designing and facilitating leadership development programs, fellowships, executive education, and/or technical assistance.
Demonstrated success securing philanthropic funding and managing donor relationships.
Familiarity with higher education-based organizations working at the intersection of research, practice, and policy.
Experience working with multiple constituencies, including community-based non-profit organizations, philanthropies and major donors, academic partners, and for-profit businesses.
Ability to work with a team effectively across different projects and work streams.
Ability to work independently, analyze problems, and develop practical solutions.

Additional Information

Appointment End Date:
Standard Hours/Schedule: 35 hours per week
Compensation Range/Rate (including Shift Differential if applicable):
Visa Sponsorship Information: Harvard University is unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position
Pre-Employment Screening: Identity and Education

Work Format Details

This position has been determined by school or unit leaders that some of the duties and responsibilities can be effectively performed at a non-Harvard location. The work schedule and location will be set by the department at its discretion and based upon operational needs. When not working at a Harvard or Harvard-designated location, employees in hybrid positions must work in a Harvard registered state in compliance with the University’s Policy on Employment Outside of Massachusetts. Additional details will be discussed during the interview process. Certain visa types and funding sources may limit work location. Individuals must meet work location sponsorship requirements prior to employment.

Salary Grade and Ranges

This position is salary grade level 060. Please visit  Harvard's Salary Ranges  to view the corresponding salary range and related information.

Benefits

Harvard offers a comprehensive benefits package that is designed to support a healthy work-life balance and your physical, mental and financial wellbeing. Because here, you are what matters. Our benefits include, but are not limited to:

Generous paid time off including parental leave
Medical, dental, and vision health insurance coverage starting on day one
Retirement plans with university contributions
Wellbeing and mental health resources
Support for families and caregivers
Professional development opportunities including tuition assistance and reimbursement
Commuter benefits, discounts and campus perks

Learn more about these and additional benefits on our Benefits & Wellbeing Page.

EEO/Non-Discrimination Commitment Statement

Harvard University is committed to equal opportunity and non-discrimination. We seek talent from all parts of society and the world, and we strive to ensure everyone at Harvard thrives. Our differences help our community advance Harvard's academic purposes.

Harvard has an equal employment opportunity policy that outlines our commitment to prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation,

new_jobs.title: Divisional Development Director - Southwest Division
new_jobs.description:
If you are currently on Young Life staff, please do not apply through this external jobs board. To apply for internal job opportunities, log in to Workday and use the Jobs Hub. Applying externally may delay your application and require you to resubmit internally.

Young Life requires all employees to sign a Statement of Faith. We recommend you read this statement before applying for any position.

Because of Young Life’s exclusive Christian purposes of evangelism and discipleship as set forth in its Articles of Incorporation and in these Bylaws, and to reflect what has always been and will continue to be the position of Young Life, specifically the Christian belief that each and every employee and volunteer of the corporation should minister as a servant of God with the primary responsibility of proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ and, as such, is an integral part of the Christian mission and ministry of the corporation
,

Young Life shall only employ individuals or enlist volunteer leaders who: (a) profess a belief in Jesus Christ as their personal Savior and Lord; and (b) subscribe to the statements and policies required of all Young Life staff, including the Young Life Statement of Faith. Therefore, employees and volunteers of Young Life, during working and nonworking hours, shall: (i) be ready, willing and able to fulfill such ministry functions as may be required by the organization; (ii) refrain from conduct and statements that detract from the biblical standards taught and supported by Young Life, and (iii) abide by all policies and practices of Young Life including, without limitation, those related to religious belief or ministry activities.

Essential Duties & Core Responsibilities

Spiritual Development

Develop a yearly personal growth plan that fosters a vibrant spiritual life including time for solitude, retreat, reflection, study, prayer, and worship.
Ensure that all Young Life work is designed and carried out with an awareness of and in alignment with Young Life’s Values and Methods.
Develop strong spiritual disciplines and accountability considering possible distance supervision and travel schedule.

Campaign Leadership

Divisional Alignment: Partner closely with the divisional SVP to bring strategic fundraising value to the overall divisional strategy.
Campaign Management: Oversee, plan, and execute all supporting activities for the campaign, including timeline management, donor events, communication strategies, and stewardship.
Case for Support: Oversee the creation, implementation, and maintenance of an up-to-date campaign “Case for Support,” ensuring it includes both “give now” (current) and “give in the future”(legacy) options.
Field Collaboration: In partnership with Global Field Development, provide strategic counsel to regional and area directors in their local campaigns,building trust and alignment between development and field ministry.

Portfolio Management; Relational Fundraising

Major Gift Solicitation & Pipeline Growth: Actively manage a portfolio of approximately 125 prospects, moving them through the relationship-centered engagement pipeline. This includes an intentional focus on donor discovery, qualification, and bringing new, untapped major gift prospects into the Southwest Division pipeline.
Donor Engagement: Plan travel and schedule appointments for discovery, qualification, and major gift asks.
Gift Planning: Coordinate with the Executive Director of YL Foundation and partners to utilize both current and planned giving structures to meet donor needs and campaign goals.

Volunteer Committee Management

Strategic ECC Partnership: Partner directly with any active volunteer Executive Campaign Committee (ECC) and its Chair to shape, refine, and align divisional ministry priorities with overarching fundraising strategies.
Impact Accountability & Reporting: Establish a robust feedback loop for the ECC; design and deliver comprehensive impact reports that transparently communicate how investments are advancing the division and achieving campaign milestones.

Database, Analytics & Reporting

CRM Accountability: Maintain strict data integrity within Young Life systems. Ensure all visits, calls, and next-step strategies are logged immediately.

Donor Stewardship & Community Culture

Stewardship Strategy: Design and implement a timely stewardship program to ensure all donors are thanked and recognized appropriately.

Education & Experience

Education: Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in a job related major field of study.
Relational Fundraising Track Record: 5+ years of fundraising experience with a proven track record of managing a portfolio and closing major gifts ($50k+). Must demonstrate a relational development approach where meeting metrics never compromises authentic, pastoral care for the donor.
Ministry & Cultural Fluency: Proven ability to understand, embody, and deeply articulate the ministry and the heart of the Young Life mission today.
Professional Experience: Minimum 10 years of relevant professional experience, including leading and managing complex initiatives or programs with outcome-focused deliverables.
Leadership Experience: Minimum 3 years of direct or indirect management experience overseeing, coaching, and inspiring teams of staff or high-level volunteer committees.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

Communication & Relational Excellence: Exceptional communication skills. The ability to speak, listen, and care for high-level donors, alumni, executives, and volunteers in a way that establishes a deep, authentic connection—ensuring partners feel valued for who they are, not just what they can give.
Mission Storytelling: A gifted communicator who can passionately and authentically convey the heart of Young Life, translating how the mission is adapting and changing to meet the needs of today’s kids.
Proactive Execution: Tested and proven in environments that demand high proactivity, and strict deadline management, balanced with a collaborative team spirit.
Strategic & Financial Acumen: Ability to research prospects, build comprehensive presentation materials, and accurately manage campaign budgets.
Technical Proficiency: Experienced in utilizing CRM databases to track metrics and drive strategic decisions.
Spiritual Integrity & Confidentiality: Absolute commitment to maintaining donor confidentiality and practicing spiritual integrity in fundraising, viewing development as a form of ministry.

Travel & Working Conditions

Travel Required: Travel required across the division to conduct face-to-face donor visits and attend regional events.
Preferred Location: Residing within the specific division(s) assigned, working remotely.

True Soft Ventures is an information technology and services company headquartered in Dwarka, Delhi, India. The company focuses on delivering technology solutions and services that support clients in improving efficiency, scalability, and digital transformation. Operating from its base at Kautilya Apartments, True Soft Ventures serves a diverse client portfolio with tailored IT offerings. The organization values innovation, reliability, and collaboration, and provides an environment where leaders can shape strategic direction and long-term growth. Prospective team members can expect to work in a dynamic setting that emphasizes impact, accountability, and continuous improvement.
Role Description
The Exceptional Chief Executive Officer will provide overall strategic leadership, set organizational vision, and drive sustainable growth for True Soft Ventures. In this full-time remote role, the CEO will oversee company operations, financial performance, and business planning, ensuring alignment with long-term goals and market opportunities. Daily responsibilities include guiding senior leaders, making data-informed decisions, managing key stakeholder relationships, and representing the company with clients, partners, and investors. The CEO will lead strategic planning efforts, monitor sales and revenue targets, and foster a culture of innovation, inclusion, and high performance. This role requires proactive communication, clear prioritization, and the ability to steer the organization through evolving technological and market landscapes.
Qualifications

Company Description
The Centrifuge is a digital magazine created by high school students for high school students, focusing on science, math, and big ideas. The publication aims to make complex topics accessible, engaging, and relevant to young learners while encouraging curiosity and critical thinking. Content is driven by student perspectives, offering peer-to-peer insights into emerging research, foundational concepts, and broader societal implications of scientific progress. The Centrifuge values accuracy, creativity, and inclusivity, providing a platform where diverse voices can explore truth through evidence-based storytelling.

The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) will provide overall strategic and operational leadership for The Centrifuge, ensuring the magazine’s mission, financial health, and long-term growth. Responsibilities include setting and executing strategic priorities, managing budgets and resource allocation, and developing business plans that support sustainable expansion. new_jobs.description:
MOUNTAINTOP SEARCH M I S S I O N C R I T I C A L S T A F F I N G MOUNTAINTOP SEARCH | MISSION CRITICAL STAFFING

ACTIVE SEARCH | LOUISIANA (STATEWIDE) | FULL-TIME Director of Development Build a fundraising engine from the ground up for Louisiana’s wildlife, fisheries, and wild places.
COMPENSATION | LOCATION | REPORTS TO | TRAVEL

$115K–$130K | Anywhere in Louisiana | ED & Board Extensive | in-state

ABOUT THE FOUNDATION
The Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Foundation (LAWFF) exists to enhance and encourage public enjoyment and responsible use of the state’s wildlife and fisheries resources. The Foundation advances the work of the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries through youth recruitment, environmental education and training, and habitat conservation. It is a small, nimble organization with an outsized mission. To date, fundraising has focused on events and grassroots efforts; the Foundation is now ready to build a modern development program—and this role will lead that work.

WHY THIS ROLE IS WORTH A LOOK: This is a rare greenfield opportunity: the first true development leader at LAWFF, with the mandate to design the fundraising strategy, launch a major gifts program, and grow corporate and individual support across Louisiana. The Board is engaged and willing to open doors and make introductions. This leader brings the strategy and relationships to turn those doors into lasting support. For someone who loves the outdoors and believes in conserving Louisiana’s natural heritage, it is a chance to be the face and voice of the mission — and to build something that lasts. There is real room to grow with the Foundation over time.

WHAT YOU’LL DO ▪ Build and lead the Foundation’s fundraising program — strategy, structure, and execution. ▪ Develop and manage a robust pipeline of individual, corporate, and institutional donors. ▪ Launch and expand a major gifts program in partnership with Board members and key stakeholders. ▪ Collaborate on events and campaigns to maximize revenue and donor engagement. ▪ Raise awareness, visibility, and support for LAWFF across the state as its chief ambassador. ▪ Serve as a strategic partner to the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries. ▪ Track, measure, and report on fundraising performance and growth.

THE PROFILE More than a fundraiser — the chief ambassador, storyteller, and advocate for the Foundation. We’re open to seasoned development leaders and to strong relationship-builders from adjacent fields (corporate partnerships, business development, sales) who bring genuine fundraising instincts and a real passion for conservation.

WHAT YOU BRING (REQUIRED) NICE TO HAVE (PREFERRED) ▪ 4+ years in fundraising, development, or a related field, with demonstrated major-gifts success. ▪ Proven ability to build relationships and influence diverse stakeholders — and to make the ask. ▪ Excellent written and verbal communication; presence ▪ 10+ years in development, ideally having built or scaled a program from the ground up. ▪ Major gifts, planned giving, and comprehensive campaign experience. ▪ Knowledge of Louisiana’s philanthropic landscape and credibility with donors and partners. ▪ Highly resourceful, self-directed, and results-oriented. ▪ Genuine passion for conservation, wildlife, and the outdoors. ▪ Bachelor’s degree; willingness to travel extensively within Louisiana. donor community. ▪ Corporate sponsorship and partnership development. ▪ Comfort as the public face — speaking to audiences large and small. ▪ Background in conservation, outdoor, or athletes’ organizations.

Our client is an Equal Opportunity Employer and values diversity in its workforce. Reasonable accommodations are available upon request.
